title: amphibromus quadridentulus
intro: Amphibromus quadridentulus, also known as sea grass or sea grass of the hills, is a type of flowering plant in the grass family that is native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health issues such as wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Amphibromus quadridentulus include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and improve skin health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, and are often used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
